MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

James Huan Ly; Lorber, Greenfield & Polito LLP; 415-986-0688; appearing in person on behalf of defendants Mitchell Huang and Deborah Min.
Matthew R. Bradley; Porter | Scott, APC; 906-929-1481; appearing via Zoom on behalf of cross-defendants Park North Real Estate and Courtney Davis.
P. Kurt Peterson; Peterson, Martin & Reynolds, LLP; 415-399-2900; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiff Constance Finley.

Court reporter: Teresa Kenworthy, CSR # 6673, tkenworthy@gmail.com, 530-308-2691.

Tentative ruling contested by cross-defendants Courtney Davis and Park North Real Estate, and plaintiff Constance Finley. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS COURTNEY DAVIS AND PARK NORTH REAL ESTATE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Motion is denied as to the first and the third issues, triable issues of fact as to the gravamen of the complaint exist. The applicable statute of limitations depends on the nature of the wrongdoing - negligence vs. fraud. Since there is a factual dispute on this action that will need to be resolved before the applicable statute of limitations may be determined, the issue of statute of limitations cannot be determined as a matter of law. Motion is granted as to the second issue, no triable issues of fact created as to the taking, retaining or assisting in taking/retaining elder's property. Evidence of broker's/agent's involvement after Plaintiff turned 65 years old does not support Plaintiff's theory that these defendants either directly took/retained or assisted in taking/retaining elder's property. (Plaintiff's declaration, paragraph 20 and Ex. L).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Teresa Kenworthy, CSR # 6673, tkenworthy@gmail.com, 530-308-2691. (D501)

Off Calendar Jul-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT COURTNEY DAVIS, PARK NORTH REAL ESTATE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action for indemnity and contribution for Plaintiff to specify by page and line number claims asserted by Plaintiff in the First Amended Complaint alleged against Pacific Union/Hatvany in their alleged capacity as buyer's agent/broker (as opposed to their capacity as seller's agent/broker), which are now being re-asserted against the cross-defendants; and (2) if Pacific Union/Hatvany asserts that seller's agent/broker is entitled to indemnity by buyer's agent/broker, allege facts in support of elements required to state such a claim (case authority with similar factual posture, i.e. non-disclosure/concealment of material facts by buyer's agent/broker should be located and provided to Cross-Defendants in the next round of good faith meet and confer, if necessary). As to the cause of action for declaratory relief, the Court has previously ordered Cross-Complaint to allege only non-duplicative causes of action. Cross-Complainants concede that their declaratory relief cause of action is an indemnity cause of action. (Opposition 12:15-16), and therefore is duplicative. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
